title: Widespread global exacerbation of extreme drought induced by urbanization

data_scope.spatial_coverage: global city sites from worldwide weather-station observations
data_scope.future_scenarios: CMIP6 mid-21st-century plus projected urban expansion

core_conclusion: Global weather-station observations show urbanization intensifies local drought: drought severity increased at ~36% of global city sites and extreme SPEI at ~43%. Paired WRF simulations attribute this to warmer, drier urban environments that suppress light rainfall. CMIP6 mid-century projections indicate ~57% and ~70% of urban regions would face worsened drought severity and extreme SPEI from continued urban expansion.
why_it_matters: Among the first global, observation-based demonstrations that urbanization worsens extreme local drought; pairs detection with WRF-based mechanism and CMIP6 projection; argues for explicit urban drought preparedness under joint urbanization, population growth and climate change.
why_nature: Global transferable signal rather than a single case; integrates in-situ data, a process model, and future scenarios; surprising agenda-shifting claim that cities drive rainfall extremes; high urban water-security and adaptation relevance.
added_value_to_my_research: Explainable ML on urban-form covariates -> attributable continuous drought-risk surface
Physics-informed station-graph interpolation -> gap-filled uncertainty-aware SPEI field
Overlay drought map with water/primary-care access -> 2SFCA-style vulnerability index
Multi-source fusion of RS/mobile/social -> morphology-resolved urbanization covariates
FAIR urban-drought benchmark -> corpus to fine-tune a geospatial foundation model
